text.title: Speech-to-Text Advanced Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failures in advanced transcription pipelines.

antipattern.symptom: "Speakers" labelled by pause-detection heuristic; one speaker labelled as 3 across a single utterance.
antipattern.root-cause: Diarisation done in code over a non-diarising provider (Whisper) instead of using native diarisation.
antipattern.fix: Use Deepgram or AssemblyAI with diarize=true; remove the heuristic (rule r1).

antipattern.root-cause: Vocab list concatenated into the system prompt instead of passed as a provider parameter.
antipattern.fix: Pass keywords / hotwords / vocab_boost via the provider API param; remove from prompt (rule r3).

antipattern.symptom: A two-speaker meeting produces 5 distinct "Speaker N" labels.
antipattern.root-cause: No speaker_hint count passed; provider over-segments.
antipattern.fix: Pass max_speakers / n_speakers based on calendar / metadata (rule r5).

antipattern.symptom: Captions cannot align to lip motion; word-level timestamps are absent.
antipattern.root-cause: Word timestamps not requested; segment-level start/end used to interpolate.
antipattern.fix: Pass word_timestamps=true (or equivalent provider flag); use actual per-word timing (rule r2).

antipattern.symptom: Raw transcript shows "uh", "um", missing punctuation, fully expanded numbers.
antipattern.fix: Run punctuation restoration + filler removal + abbreviation policy as post-process (rule r6).
